description: MT-RNR1 (mitochondrial 12S rRNA) aminoglycoside-ototoxicity panel: m.1555A>G (rs267606617; ref A, risk G), m.1494C>T (rs267606619; ref C, risk T), and m.1095T>C (rs267606618; ref T, risk C). Per the 2021 CPIC guideline, carriers of m.1555A>G or m.1494C>T are at high risk of permanent aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss and should avoid aminoglycosides where a safe, effective alternative exists; m.1095T>C is a preliminary/lower-evidence association. Mitochondrial DNA is maternally inherited and these variants are typically homoplasmic, but arrays report a single call and cannot measure heteroplasmy. These positions are frequently off-chip on consumer arrays (off_chip_risk=high); an untyped/absent probe is surfaced as indeterminate, never a false-negative. Not ClinVar P/LP in the nuclear sense -> clinvar_significance=NULL. CPIC guidance is decision-support, not a prescription; confirm with clinical mitochondrial testing. EXPANSION_STRATEGY.md §9 / roadmap #55.

genotype_models.risk_classification: MT-RNR1 m.1555A>G — high risk of aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss
genotype_models.finding_text: MT-RNR1 m.1555A>G detected (rs267606617 {genotype}). This is the best-established mitochondrial variant for aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss. The 2021 CPIC guideline recommends that carriers avoid aminoglycoside antibiotics (e.g. gentamicin, tobramycin, amikacin, streptomycin) unless the severity of an infection and the lack of a safe, effective alternative outweigh the high risk of permanent hearing loss. This is genetic decision-support to discuss with a clinician or pharmacist — not an instruction to start or stop any medication.

genotype_models.risk_classification: MT-RNR1 m.1494C>T — high risk of aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss
genotype_models.finding_text: MT-RNR1 m.1494C>T detected (rs267606619 {genotype}). Like m.1555A>G, this variant makes the mitochondrial 12S rRNA resemble its bacterial target, and family studies link aminoglycoside exposure in carriers to sensorineural hearing loss. The 2021 CPIC guideline recommends that carriers avoid aminoglycoside antibiotics unless a severe infection and the lack of a safe, effective alternative outweigh the high risk of permanent hearing loss. This is genetic decision-support to discuss with a clinician or pharmacist — not an instruction to start or stop any medication.

MT-RNR1 m.1095T>C detected (rs267606618 {genotype}). This variant may be associated with aminoglycoside-related sensorineural hearing loss, but the evidence is weaker than for m.1555A>G/m.1494C>T and further study is needed. CPIC still advises caution with aminoglycosides for carriers. Treat this as preliminary context to raise with a clinician or pharmacist, not a confirmed high-risk result.
